ddls:
CREATE TABLE "schools" (
  "school_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school */,
  "school_name" TEXT /* Name of the school */,
  "school_type" TEXT /* Type of school (e.g., Community School, Academy) */,
  "local_authority" TEXT /* Local authority responsible for the school */,
  "urn" INTEGER /* URN (Unique Reference Number) of the school */,
  "address" TEXT /* Full address of the school */,
  "postcode" TEXT /* Postcode of the school */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the school */,
  "email" TEXT /* Contact email address of the school */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL of the school's website */,
  "established_year" INTEGER /* Year the school was established */,
  "headteacher" TEXT /* Name of the headteacher */,
  "number_of_students" INTEGER /* Number of students enrolled in the school */,
  "number_of_teachers" INTEGER /* Number of teachers at the school */,
  "geolocation" TEXT /* Geolocation coordinates (e.g., latitude and longitude) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "qualifications" (
  "qualification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each qualification */,
  "qualification_name" TEXT /* Name of the qualification (e.g., GCE A level, GCSE) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the qualification */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the qualification (e.g., 2 years) */,
  "level" TEXT /* Level of the qualification (e.g., Level 3) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("qualification_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject (e.g., Mathematics, Drama & Theatre Studies) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the subject */,
  "subject_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the subject */,
  "subject_type" TEXT /* Type of the subject (e.g., Core, Elective) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "examination_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each examination result */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school */,
  "qualification_id" INTEGER /* ID of the qualification */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subject */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the examination */,
  "a_star" INTEGER /* Number of A* grades */,
  "a" INTEGER /* Number of A grades */,
  "b" INTEGER /* Number of B grades */,
  "c" INTEGER /* Number of C grades */,
  "d" INTEGER /* Number of D grades */,
  "e" INTEGER /* Number of E grades */,
  "fail_no_results" INTEGER /* Number of failures or no results */,
  "total_entries" INTEGER /* Total number of entries */,
  "average_grade" REAL /* Average grade for the subject */,
  "pass_rate" REAL /* Percentage of students passing the examination */,
  "distinction_rate" REAL /* Percentage of students achieving distinction */,
  "gender_distribution" TEXT /* JSON object representing the gender distribution of students */,
  "ethnicity_distribution" TEXT /* JSON object representing the ethnicity distribution of students */,
  "socioeconomic_status_distribution" TEXT /* JSON object representing the socioeconomic status distribution of students */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_examination_results_qualification_id FOREIGN KEY ("qualification_id") REFERENCES qualifications ("qualification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_examination_results_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_examination_results_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "local_authorities" (
  "authority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each local authority */,
  "authority_name" TEXT /* Name of the local authority */,
  "address" TEXT /* Full address of the local authority */,
  "postcode" TEXT /* Postcode of the local authority */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the local authority */,
  "email" TEXT /* Contact email address of the local authority */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL of the local authority's website */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("authority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "school_administrators" (
  "admin_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each school administrator */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school */,
  "admin_name" TEXT /* Name of the administrator */,
  "admin_email" TEXT /* Email address of the administrator */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position of the administrator (e.g., Principal, Vice Principal) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the administrator */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("admin_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_school_administrators_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_school_administrators_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teachers" (
  "teacher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each teacher */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school */,
  "teacher_name" TEXT /* Name of the teacher */,
  "teacher_email" TEXT /* Email address of the teacher */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position of the teacher (e.g., Head of Department, Class Teacher) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the teacher */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subject the teacher specializes in */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("teacher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teachers_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teachers_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_teachers_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "students" (
  "student_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each student */,
  "school_id" INTEGER /* ID of the school */,
  "student_name" TEXT /* Name of the student */,
  "student_email" TEXT /* Email address of the student */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the student */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the student */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the student */,
  "socioeconomic_status" TEXT /* Socioeconomic status of the student */,
  "class" TEXT /* Class or grade of the student */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("student_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_school_id FOREIGN KEY ("school_id") REFERENCES schools ("school_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_students_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object representing the permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
